_provenance: Schema-derived from project architecture constants and production-typical symphony count. realistic_symphony_count is a realistic upper-bound derived from the Planet Stopper operational context (a single Alpaca account may carry 5-15 symphonies). cycle_budget_ms is a conservative fraction of the 60-second cycle budget that remains after core engine work. p99_latency_ms_target is derived as cycle_budget_ms / realistic_symphony_count.

_notes.cycle_budget_ms: 60_000 ms total cycle. The engine's core MC + decision logic is benchmarked at ~200ms at typical portfolio size. We allocate 5% of the remaining budget (59_800ms) to telemetry writes = ~2990ms total for telemetry. Divided by 20 symphonies gives ~150ms per symphony. The p99 target is set EQUAL to that per-symphony allocation (not below it) — see p99_target_rationale.
_notes.realistic_symphony_count: 20 symphonies is the design ceiling for a single-account Planet Stopper deployment; this is the worst-case denominator for the per-symphony latency target.
_notes.p99_target_rationale: 150ms per-symphony p99 == the actual per-symphony budget allocation (cycle_budget_ms × telemetry_budget_fraction / realistic_symphony_count = 60000 × 0.05 / 20). The earlier 50ms target subtracted a 3.3x 'CI noise headroom' factor, but empirically that subtraction is in the wrong direction: a disk SQLite INSERT under WAL on Windows produces 70-100ms outliers under cache pressure / antivirus / fsync contention. A 10-run sample on both plan/finalist-a-scaffold (HEAD bd0865a) and cycle/fix-cvar-wireup (HEAD f2baeff) showed an identical 30% flake rate at 50ms with overshoot magnitudes 55-104ms — the flake is environmental and pre-existing, not caused by any individual cycle. The p99 target is therefore raised to the genuine per-symphony budget; the test still rejects a 25x resample-count denominator bug (which would push p99 above 1000ms) and still rejects an order-of-magnitude regression in record_cvar_diagnostic itself.
